zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert de Praers於大約1183年出生在英國爾達頓時，他的父親Sir Adam de Praers18歲，母親Amelia Lancelyn14歲。他於1325年與Mrs. Robert de Praers在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1279年享年97歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert de Praers于大约1183年出生在英国爾達頓时，他的父亲Sir Adam de Praers18岁，母亲Amelia Lancelyn14岁。他于1325年与Mrs. Robert de Praers在英国英格兰爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1279年享年97岁。

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert de Praers was born about 1183, in Dutton, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir Adam de Praers, was 18 and his mother, Amelia Lancelyn, was 14. He married Mrs. Robert de Praers in 1325, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1279, at the age of 97.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert de Praers est né environ 1183, à Dutton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Adam de Praers, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Amelia Lancelyn, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Mrs. Robert de Praers en 1325, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1279, à l’âge de 97 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert de Praers um 1183 in Dutton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Adam de Praers 18 und seine Mutter Amelia Lancelyn 14. Er hat 1325 in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Mrs. Robert de Praers geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1279 im Alter von 97 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert de Praers è nato il circa 1183 nel Dutton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Adam de Praers, aveva 18 anni e il suo madre, Amelia Lancelyn, aveva 14 anni. Lui sposato Mrs. Robert de Praers nel 1325, nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1279, all'età di 97 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Praersが約1183年にイギリスチェシュアイアダットオンで生まれた時，父Sir Adam de Praersは18歳，母Amelia Lancelynは14歳であった。1325年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアでMrs. Robert de Praersと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1279年に97歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Praers(이)가 대략 1183년 영국 체실르 드턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Adam de Praers(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Amelia Lancelyn(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1325년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Mrs. Robert de Praers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1279년 97세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert de Praers nasceu aproximadamente 1183, em Dutton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Adam de Praers, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Amelia Lancelyn, tinha 14 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Robert de Praers em 1325, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1279, com 97 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert de Praers родился около 1183 г. в местности дуттон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Adam de Praers было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Amelia Lancelyn, 14. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Robert de Praers в 1325 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1279 г. в возрасте 97 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert de Praers nació aproximadamente 1183, en Dutton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Adam de Praers, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Amelia Lancelyn, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Mrs. Robert de Praers en 1325, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1279, a la edad de 97 años.
